channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Body recovered in Gaza confirmed to be 23-year-old Hamzah AlZayadni
item.description: Israel’s army confirmed Friday that one of the bodies recovered from Gaza earlier this week was that of 23-year-old hostage Hamzah AlZayadni. His body was retrieved with that of his father, Yosef AlZayadni, who were taken together when Hamas-led militants stormed into southern Israel 15 months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tanker hit by Houthi rebels that threatened a Red Sea oil spill has been salvaged
item.description: An oil tanker that burned for weeks in the Red Sea and threatened a massive oil spill has been “successfully” salvaged.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Moldovans grapple with outages as energy crisis grips pro-Russia region
item.description: Every time planned electrical outages are imposed on his village in southern Moldova, 73-year-old retiree Vasili Donici passes the time by solving crosswords and puzzles in a room he illuminates using a small gas lamp.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Mcgrath And Aurel Obreja, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Estonia steps up patrols in the icy Baltic Sea in a show of force after suspected cable sabotage
item.description: Estonian naval ships are taking part in stepped-up patrols in the Baltic Sea by NATO countries after undersea power and communications cables have been damaged in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows And Hendrik Osula, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Canada's Liberal Party to decide who will be the country's next prime minister on March 9
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's governing Liberal Party will announce the country's next prime minister on March 9 after a leadership vote that follows the resignation of Justin Trudeau this week, party leaders said late Thursday. Trudeau remains prime minister until a new leader is chosen.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An albatross couple shares egg duty in this captivating low-drama reality show
item.description: A popular New Zealand bird “reality show” that each year follows a pair of northern royal albatrosses as they breed and raise a chick has drawn a global audience of devoted fans in its current 10th season.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea’s acting leader accepts resignation of presidential security chief
item.description: South Korea’s acting leader has accepted the resignation of the chief of the presidential security service, Park Jong-joon, as he faced police questioning over how his forces blocked law enforcement efforts to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol last week.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK foreign policy chief says Trump is right to urge higher military spending from NATO
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Europe’s security “is on a knife-edge” and President-elect Donald Trump is right to say NATO member nations must increase military spending, Britain’s top diplomat said Thursday. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said that “the post-Cold War peace is well and truly over.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader detained ahead of Maduro's inauguration
item.description: Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado was detained amid antigovernment protests in Caracas on Thursday, a day before President Nicolás Maduro is expected to be sworn in to a third full term in office.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Thousands of Venezuelan opposition supporters take to the streets ahead of Maduro's third inauguration
item.description: Venezuelan opposition supporters protested around the country in a last-minute effort to put pressure on President Maduro the day before he is to be sworn in for his third term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, Gaza's Health Ministry said Thursday, with no end in sight to the 15-month conflict. The ministry said a total of 46,006 Palestinians have been killed and 109,378 wounded.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia monitoring Trump’s ‘dramatic’ comments on Greenland acquisition
item.description: The Kremlin said Thursday they are monitoring President-elect Donald Trump's "dramatic" comments over his desire to acquire Greenland amid other expansionist remarks, including the Panama Canal and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NOAA says La Nina ocean cooling has finally arrived, but it's weak and may cause fewer problems
item.description: A long-awaited La Nina has finally appeared, but the periodic cooling of Pacific Ocean waters is weak and unlikely to cause as many weather problems as usual, meteorologists said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Filipino Catholics pray for good health and peace in huge procession venerating Jesus statue
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Some 1.8 million mostly barefoot Catholic worshippers marched Thursday in an annual procession in the Philippines that venerates a centuries-old black statue of Jesus.
item.dc:creator: TERESA CEROJANO AND JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italy's Meloni hopes to attend Trump inauguration as she downplays his Greenland and Panama comments
item.description: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni says she doesn't believe President-elect Donald Trump actually intends to use military force to seize control of Greenland or the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mozambique opposition leader returns from self-imposed exile as police fire tear gas at supporters
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique's main opposition leader returned from self-imposed exile on Thursday clutching a bible and saying he still rejects the results of a disputed election last year that has sparked more than two months of protests and a violent crackdown by security forces.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attack on Chad's presidential palace leaves 19 dead
item.description: N'DJAMENA, Chad (AP) — An attack on Chad's presidential palace left 18 assailants dead and six in custody, with one soldier killed and three wounded, state media reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope asks aide to deliver his annual foreign policy address, tells ambassadors he's battling a cold
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis told ambassadors accredited to the Holy See on Thursday that he wasn’t up to delivering his annual foreign policy speech and asked an aide to deliver it instead. A congested-sounding Francis began the speech, greeting the ambassadors gathered in the Hall of Blessings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's Ishiba visiting Malaysia and Indonesia to strengthen defense and economic ties
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba is visiting Malaysia and Indonesia as part of his effort to further strengthen defense and economic ties with Southeast Asia as threats from China rise in the region.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An Italian journalist is freed from detention in Iran and returns home
item.description: ROME (AP) — An Italian journalist detained in Iran for three weeks was freed Wednesday and returned home, after her fate had become intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer arrested in Italy and wanted by the United States.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Russian missile attack in southern Ukraine has killed at least 13 civilians, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A daytime Russian missile attack on the southern Ukraine city of Zaporizhzhia killed at least 13 civilians and wounded about 30 others on Wednesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Americans arrested in Venezuela on eve of Maduro inauguration over ‘terrorism’ claims
item.description: Two Americans have been arrested in Venezuela, with President Nicolas Maduro claiming the charges relate to an intent to "practice terrorism against the Venezuelan people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: 23-year-old hiker found after surviving for 2 weeks in Australian mountain range
item.description: A medical student from Melbourne who was missing in a remote Australian mountain range for two weeks survived on two muesli bars, foraged berries and creek water, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US strikes underground Houthi weapons depots used to hit American ships
item.description: U.S. hit weapon storage facilities used by the Iran-backed Houthi terrorist organization to target U.S. military and merchant ships in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico offers protection to famed singer after drug cartel death threats
item.description: Mexican authorities are offering protection to famed regional Mexican singer Natanael Cano and other artists after a drug cartel publicly threatened them, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian journalist Cecilia Sala freed from detention in Iran
item.description: Italian journalist Cecilia Sala was released after being detained in Iran for three weeks. Sala's plight became intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer wanted by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trudeau says there ‘isn’t a snowball’s chance in hell’ Canada will become part of US
item.description: Canadian prime minister Justin Trudeau said in an X post on Tuesday that there is not a "snowball's chance in hell" Canada will become part of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Controversial founder of French far right, Jean-Marie Le Pen, dies aged 96
item.description: Jean-Marie Le Pen, who founded France's far-right National Front party, died at 96 years old. Le Pen was a controversial and confrontational figure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader known for fiery rhetoric against immigration, dies at 96
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s far-right National Front who was known for fiery rhetoric against immigration and multiculturalism that earned him both staunch supporters and widespread condemnation, died Tuesday. He was 96.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, SYLVIE CORBET and ELAINE GANLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation
